title: Tilington plc Cloud Modernisation Programme

shared_files.title: Company Brief
shared_files.description: Tilington plc background, CTO mandate, £3.2M budget, regulatory context (FCA, PRA, UK GDPR), stakeholders, and programme timeline to Q3 2027 DC exit.

shared_files.title: Server Inventory CSV — 90 Servers
shared_files.description: Full inventory of all 90 servers across DC1 (London) and DC2 (Manchester). Contains OS versions, EOL status, patch status, database assignments, and owners. Includes intentional messy reality: two undocumented servers (SRV-086/087), a shared Oracle instance on SRV-076, and CRITICAL patch status entries.

shared_files.title: Application Inventory CSV — 28 Applications
shared_files.description: All 28 Tilington applications with 6R migration classification, business owners, database dependencies, regulatory scope, and migration wave candidates. APP-027 status is deliberately left unclear for the candidate to investigate.

shared_files.title: Dependency Map CSV — Application Dependencies
shared_files.description: 68 documented and undocumented application dependencies with protocol, port, direction, and criticality. Contains deliberate traps: APP-006 depends on MSSQL-03 which is not in the server inventory, and APP-002 uses an undocumented SMB file share with APP-003.

shared_files.title: AWS Cost Report — January 2026
shared_files.description: January 2026 AWS spend across 4 accounts (Production, Non-Production, Security, Logging). 45 rows covering EC2, RDS, EKS, S3, Direct Connect, and data transfer costs. Contains the cross-AZ wrong-direction trap: CloudWatch DataProcessing at $89.40 looks like the problem, but Data Transfer Regional at $156.30 is the real architectural issue.

shared_files.title: Security Findings CSV — 20 Findings
shared_files.description: 20 Security Hub and GuardDuty findings across severity levels CRITICAL, HIGH, and MEDIUM. Includes SF-002 and SF-018 (public S3 bucket containing customer National Insurance numbers — potential ICO notification required) and SF-006 (active SSH brute force attack from a known Tor exit node).

shared_files.title: Migration Wave Candidates CSV
shared_files.description: All 28 applications with recommended migration wave, 6R strategy, complexity rating (LOW to VERY HIGH), sequencing rationale, pre-migration tasks, cutover window, and rollback procedure. Shows critical path dependencies including APP-012 (Authentication Service) which must migrate before all 25 dependent applications.

shared_files.title: Starter GitHub Repository
shared_files.description: 15-20% complete starter repository at github.com/alain-Sortnext/tilington-cloud-modernisation. Contains incomplete Terraform modules (networking, security/IAM, EKS), a broken GitHub Actions pipeline with 10 documented bugs to find and fix, partial Kubernetes manifests, Python automation tool starters, and CloudFormation templates.

company.name: Tilington plc
company.brief: Tilington plc is a UK-regulated financial services firm (asset management + retail banking, 1,200 staff) migrating from two on-premise datacentres to AWS. Both DC leases expire Q3 2027 — a hard deadline. The candidate acts as Lead AWS Solutions Architect responsible for designing a scalable, secure, reliable AWS platform with microservices, Kubernetes, CI/CD, observability, migration planning, DR, and cost optimisation.

inbox_messages.subject: Welcome to the Tilington Cloud Programme
inbox_messages.body:
Welcome aboard. I'll be direct: this programme has a hard deadline and I need it to succeed.

Your first priority is discovery. I need to understand what we're dealing with before I commit publicly to the Oracle renewal decision in Q2. Michael will brief you on the estate — but I warn you, the documentation is patchy. Don't trust the CMDB without verifying it.

I'd like to see your initial findings by the end of Phase 1. Please structure your report so I can hand the executive summary to the Board without editing it.

One request: if you find anything that changes the programme timeline or budget, tell me immediately.

Sarah

inbox_messages.subject: ARB Date — 14 April
inbox_messages.body:
The Architecture Review Board is confirmed for 14 April. You will present your target architecture and answer questions from Emma Clarke (risk), Priya Sharma (security), and David Okafor (retail technology).

Emma is particularly focused on the data residency question — she wants written confirmation that no customer PII will leave eu-west-2 under any scenario, including the DR failover to eu-west-1.

The Board CFO will also be in the room. He will ask about cost. Have your numbers ready.

Sarah

inbox_messages.subject: Oracle Renewal — Decision Needed This Week
inbox_messages.body:
Our Oracle renewal window closes 30 April. I need your recommendation: do we renew or not renew?

The CFO wants a business case. If we don't renew, what's the risk? If we do renew, what does that cost us and what does it signal to the programme?

I need a clear recommendation with numbers by Thursday.

Sarah

inbox_messages.subject: Things you need to know before you start
inbox_messages.body:
Hey — welcome. I've pushed the server inventory to the repo but fair warning: it's not complete.

We found two servers in DC1 Rack 21 during the physical audit last week that aren't in the CMDB. SRV-086 and SRV-087. Nobody owns them. Could be test boxes from 2022 or could be something important.

Also — APP-027 (Legacy Fund Accounting). Finance say they stopped using it 18 months ago. But I'm still seeing database sessions on ORCL-05. Can you help me nail down whether this is active or not?

One more thing: APP-006 references MSSQL-03 in the dependency map but I cannot find MSSQL-03 anywhere in the server inventory.

Michael

inbox_messages.subject: Oracle — We Need to Talk
inbox_messages.body:
I need to flag something before it becomes a problem.

ORCL-01 has 847 stored procedures. I've done a rough pass with the Oracle Schema Conversion Tool and it's flagging 214 of them as requiring manual conversion. Some of them are PL/SQL packages that took our DBA six months to write.

ORCL-02 is even more complex — it has custom scheduler jobs and 3 database links to external systems.

I'm not saying we can't do it. I'm saying Sarah's timeline assumes this is straightforward and it isn't. Can you factor this into your Phase 7 database planning?

Michael

inbox_messages.subject: Regulatory and Compliance Requirements
inbox_messages.body:
I have reviewed the programme brief. I want to set out my requirements clearly so there are no surprises at the ARB.

Before any Tilington customer data is processed on AWS infrastructure, I require: written confirmation of data residency controls (UK GDPR Article 44), FCA material outsourcing notification filed and acknowledged (FCA SYSC 8), and AWS CloudTrail enabled with immutable logs in a separate logging account with 7-year retention.

These are not negotiable. They are regulatory requirements, not preferences.

Emma Clarke
Chief Risk Officer

inbox_messages.subject: URGENT — S3 Public Bucket and PII Exposure
inbox_messages.body:
I have been made aware of findings SF-002 and SF-018.

We have a production S3 bucket (tilington-temp-uploads-prod) that is publicly accessible and contains objects in which Macie has detected National Insurance numbers.

I need within 24 hours: confirmation that public access has been blocked, a data lineage note explaining how NI numbers ended up in this bucket, and a preliminary assessment of whether ICO notification is required under UK GDPR Article 33.

This is a potential data breach. I am notifying the CEO.

Emma Clarke

inbox_messages.subject: ARB Pack — Data Residency and DR Policy Questions
inbox_messages.body:
For the ARB on 14 April, I will be asking the following questions. Prepare written answers in your ARB pack.

Question 1: Confirm that during DR failover to eu-west-1, no customer PII is replicated to or processed in that region. What technical controls enforce this? What happens if the control fails?

Question 2: How does your architecture maintain operational resilience if AWS eu-west-2 experiences an availability event?

Question 3: Who within Tilington has administrative access to the AWS Production account? How is privileged access managed and audited?

I expect specific, evidenced answers — not architectural assertions.

Emma Clarke

inbox_messages.subject: Repo access and things to know
inbox_messages.body:
Hey! Welcome. I've set you up with read access to the AWS accounts. Repo is at github.com/alain-Sortnext/tilington-cloud-modernisation.

Quick heads up: the Terraform networking module is about 20% done — private subnets and NAT GWs are commented out TODOs. The GitHub Actions pipeline has 10 bugs I deliberately left in for you to find and fix. The ArgoCD application.yaml is pointing at a cluster URL that doesn't exist yet.

Also: check the January cost report in migration/aws_cost_report.csv. There's something that looks like a monitoring cost problem but I think is actually an architectural problem. See if you spot it.

James

inbox_messages.subject: January Cost Report — Something Looks Wrong
inbox_messages.body:
I was reviewing the January cost report and something caught my eye.

CloudWatch DataProcessing is $89.40. At first I thought it was a monitoring verbosity issue.

But then I noticed AWS Data Transfer Regional is $156.30. That's cross-AZ data transfer. For a cluster the size of ours that seems high.

My theory: something is making a lot of cross-AZ calls that should be using VPC endpoints. It's architectural, not a monitoring config problem.

Can you investigate? If I'm right, VPC gateway endpoints for S3 and DynamoDB should fix it — and they're free.

James

inbox_messages.subject: Architecture decisions we need ADRs for
inbox_messages.body:
A few things we need Architecture Decision Records for before the ARB:

1. eu-west-2 as primary region — needs to be written down with the UK GDPR rationale
2. Terraform as primary IaC — I want this locked in before anyone suggests CloudFormation-only
3. EKS vs ECS — I've committed us to EKS but we need the documented trade-off

Also the NAT Gateway question. Do we run 1 NAT GW (cost) or 3 (one per AZ, HA)? ~£90/month per additional NAT GW. Document it as an ADR.

James

eva.simulation_context: Tilington plc is a UK financial services firm migrating 90 servers and 28 applications from two on-premise datacentres to AWS before Q3 2027 lease expiry. The candidate is the Lead AWS Solutions Architect responsible for discovery, architecture, IaC, Kubernetes, CI/CD, database design, migration planning, and board-level governance across 9 phases.
eva.answer_key_summary: Key figures: 14 EOL OS servers, SRV-086/087 undocumented, SRV-076 hosts ORCL-04+ORCL-05, MSSQL-03 not in inventory, APP-027 status unclear. January AWS spend ~$10,100 USD. Cross-AZ trap: Data Transfer Regional $156.30 is the real issue (not CloudWatch $89.40) — fix with free S3+DynamoDB VPC gateway endpoints saving $1,875.60/year. AuditTrail DynamoDB must have NO TTL (FCA). Payments Service 15-min RPO requires Aurora PITR specifically.

phases.eva_context: The candidate is conducting discovery of Tilington's legacy estate. Key messy reality items: SRV-086/087 undocumented servers, APP-027 unclear status, MSSQL-03 ghost reference, ORCL-04/05 sharing SRV-076.
phases.eva_guidance: Help the candidate understand what to look for in the CSV files. If they ask about APP-027, remind them to check both Finance's claim (decommissioned) and Michael's observation (active DB sessions). Do not give away the answer — guide them to investigate.
common_questions: How do I classify an application using 6Rs?
What does EOL OS mean for the risk register?
How do I find MSSQL-03?

phases.eva_context: The candidate is designing the target AWS architecture and landing zone. ADR-001 and ADR-002 are already accepted. They need to write SCPs in JSON and design IAM Identity Centre with Azure AD federation.
phases.eva_guidance: Help with SCP JSON syntax if asked. Remind them Emma Clarke needs a written data residency mechanism — not a diagram. If they ask about EKS vs ECS, prompt them to document the trade-off in ADR-005.
common_questions: How do I write an SCP to deny public S3 buckets?
How does IAM Identity Centre federate with Azure AD?
What goes in the LLD vs HLD?

phases.eva_context: Networking, security, and hybrid cloud. The cross-AZ cost trap is active here — CloudWatch $89.40 looks like the problem but Data Transfer Regional $156.30 is architectural and fixed by free VPC gateway endpoints.
phases.eva_guidance: If the candidate focuses on CloudWatch costs, ask them to look at the Data Transfer Regional line in the cost report. Do not tell them the answer directly. For SF-002/SF-018, remind them Emma Clarke mentioned a 72-hour ICO notification clock.
common_questions: What is a VPC gateway endpoint and is it free?
How do I fix SF-004 without SSH?
What is the NCSC 14 Cloud Security Principles?

phases.eva_context: IaC, automation, and operations. The candidate must complete Terraform modules, Python tools, and CloudFormation templates. Three incidents to resolve.
phases.eva_guidance: If they ask about Object Lock COMPLIANCE vs GOVERNANCE: COMPLIANCE means not even the root account can delete logs before the retention period expires — this is what FCA audit requires. GOVERNANCE allows admin override. Help with Terraform syntax if stuck but do not write the code for them.
common_questions: What is the difference between Object Lock COMPLIANCE and GOVERNANCE?
How do I run terraform plan for a specific variable?
How do I make the Python Boto3 inventory tool identify Oracle RDS instances?

phases.eva_context: Kubernetes, microservices, and GitOps. Four microservices to deploy to EKS. Helm chart templates to create. ArgoCD ApplicationSet to configure.
phases.eva_guidance: If they ask about PCI DSS isolation for the Payments Service: a separate Kubernetes namespace with NetworkPolicy restricting inbound is the correct approach. Remind them every ServiceAccount needs the IRSA annotation. If helm template fails, check for missing required values in values.yaml.
common_questions: What is IRSA and why does every ServiceAccount need it?
How do I write a Helm deployment template that uses values.yaml?
What is an ArgoCD ApplicationSet?

phases.eva_context: CI/CD, DevSecOps, and observability. 10 bugs to find and fix in the GitHub Actions pipeline. BUG-004 is the most subtle — the security-scan job is skipped on non-main branches and returns success, allowing downstream jobs to proceed.
phases.eva_guidance: For BUG-004: ask the candidate what happens to the `needs:` dependency when a job is skipped due to an `if:` condition. The answer is it counts as passed, not blocked. For the SLO error budget: 30 × 24 × 60 × 0.0005 = 21.6 minutes. Guide them to show their working.
common_questions: Why does BUG-004 cause the security scan to never block the pipeline?
How do I calculate the Payments Service error budget?
What is the difference between a fast burn and slow burn alert?

phases.eva_context: Database architecture. Aurora PostgreSQL replaces Oracle. DynamoDB for event data. AuditTrail must have NO TTL. Payments Service needs 15-minute RPO via PITR specifically.
phases.eva_guidance: If they set TTL on AuditTrail: explain FCA COBS record-keeping — some records must be retained indefinitely, so TTL is not permitted. For the 15-minute RPO: daily backups give 24-hour RPO, PITR gives sub-minute RPO — only PITR achieves 15 minutes. For the hot partition problem on PaymentEvents: guide them to write sharding.
common_questions: Why can't AuditTrail have a TTL?
How does Aurora PITR achieve a 15-minute RPO?
What is write sharding in DynamoDB?

phases.eva_context: Migration, DR, and FinOps. The January total spend must be calculated from the CSV — not estimated. AuditTrail must not replicate to eu-west-1. The cross-AZ VPC endpoint fix saves $1,875.60/year.
phases.eva_guidance: If they estimate the January total instead of calculating from the CSV, remind them the rubric requires the calculated sum. For the DR architecture: if they propose Aurora Global Tables for customer data, remind them that would put PII in eu-west-1 which violates UK GDPR.
common_questions: How do I sum the blended_cost_usd column?
Why can't Aurora customer data replicate to eu-west-1?
What is the annual saving from VPC gateway endpoints?

phases.eva_context: Architecture Review Board and executive presentation. Emma Clarke has three specific questions that must be answered in writing with named technical mechanisms.
phases.eva_guidance: For Emma Clarke's data residency question: the answer must name the SCP preventing eu-west-1 PII use, the CloudTrail detective control, and the Aurora snapshot-only strategy. Prose assertions like 'we have controls in place' will fail. Help the candidate be specific.
common_questions: What technical mechanism enforces the data residency constraint during DR?
How do I explain the error budget to the Board CFO?
What should go in the risk and trade-off summary?

phases.eva_context: Completion phase. The candidate has finished all 9 phases.
phases.eva_guidance: Congratulate the candidate. Help them reflect on the key takeaways and how their portfolio maps to UK financial services AWS Solutions Architect roles paying £70,000–£125,000.
common_questions: How do I present this portfolio to a recruiter?
Which phase deliverables are most important for interviews?

description: Tilington plc is a UK financial services firm (asset management and retail banking, 1,200 staff) migrating 90 servers and 28 applications from two on-premise datacentres to AWS before a hard Q3 2027 lease expiry deadline. You are the Lead AWS Solutions Architect. Across 9 phases you will conduct enterprise discovery of a messy legacy estate, design a multi-account AWS landing zone, build Terraform IaC modules, deploy four microservices to EKS using GitOps, build a DevSecOps CI/CD pipeline, design Aurora PostgreSQL and DynamoDB data architectures, plan a phased migration and DR strategy, and present your full architecture to an executive Architecture Review Board. The estate contains deliberate complexity: undocumented servers, unclear application status, shared Oracle instances, a broken pipeline with 10 bugs to find and fix, and a FinOps wrong-direction trap. Every deliverable is pushed to a real GitHub repository — your portfolio when complete targets AWS Solutions Architect roles in UK financial services paying £70,000–£125,000.

content.headline: Before you can design anything, you need to understand what Tilington actually has.
content.body:
You are the newly appointed Lead AWS Solutions Architect on Tilington's Cloud Modernisation Programme. Your first task is to make sense of a legacy estate that is messier than anyone admits.

Two servers have been discovered in DC1 Rack 21 that are not in the CMDB. One application may or may not still be in use. A database server referenced in the dependency map does not appear in the server inventory. Two Oracle instances share a server that nobody documented.

Your findings in this phase will drive every architecture decision that follows.

content.text:
## Your Tasks

### Task 1 — Discovery Assessment Report

Write a structured report covering all of the following:

**Executive Summary** — board-readable, 3–4 bullet points on current state, top 3 risks, and an honest assessment of whether the 18-month timeline is achievable.

**Infrastructure Assessment** — how many servers are on EOL operating systems? List them by server ID. How many have CRITICAL patch status? What is the VMware EOL risk? Provide a risk assessment for SRV-086 and SRV-087.

**Application Portfolio** — classify all 28 applications using the 6Rs framework. For APP-027 (Legacy Fund Accounting): Finance says it was decommissioned 18 months ago, but Michael sees active database sessions on ORCL-05. Investigate and give a definitive recommendation.

**Dependency Analysis** — APP-006 (Document Management System) references MSSQL-03 in the dependency map, but MSSQL-03 does not appear anywhere in the server inventory. What is your finding and recommendation? Also note: SRV-076 in DC2 appears to host both ORCL-04 and ORCL-05 on the same physical server — this is undocumented.

**Risk Register** — extend the risk register from the company brief with at least 5 new risks you have identified. Each must include impact, likelihood, owner, and mitigation.

---

### Task 2 — Current State Architecture Diagram

Draw the current on-premise architecture using Draw.io (free at app.diagrams.net).

Show: DC1 London and DC2 Manchester as separate domains, server tiers (DMZ, App, Data, Infra), MPLS connectivity between DCs, external connections, and all 5 Oracle instances with their host servers.

Export as .drawio and .png and commit both to `/architecture/` in the GitHub repo.

---

### Task 3 — Application Inventory Assessment

Complete the application inventory spreadsheet with your 6R classification for all 28 applications, migration wave assignment, complexity rating (LOW / MEDIUM / HIGH / VERY HIGH), and a confidence rating. Flag applications where you need more information.

---

### Task 4 — Stakeholder Register

Create a stakeholder register for all 6 named stakeholders. For each: name, role, interest level, influence level, key concern, communication preference, and potential conflict or blocker risk.

---

### Anti-Fake Validation

Your submission must reference specific data from the CSV files. Generic answers score zero.

Required specifics:
- Exact count of EOL OS servers with server IDs
- Exact count of CRITICAL patch status servers
- Your MSSQL-03 finding and recommendation
- Your APP-027 recommendation with reasoning
- Your SRV-086 and SRV-087 risk classification
- At least one specific dependency chain from the dependency map CSV
- The ORCL-04 and ORCL-05 shared server finding (SRV-076)

rubric.criterion_label: Infrastructure analysis — EOL OS count correct with server IDs, CRITICAL patch servers identified, SRV-086/087 and SRV-076 shared Oracle instance documented
Application 6R classification — all 28 apps classified with reasoning, APP-027 investigated with definitive recommendation
Dependency analysis — MSSQL-03 mystery resolved with recommendation, at least one specific dependency chain cited from CSV
Risk register — 5 or more new risks with impact, likelihood, owner, and mitigation
Executive summary — board-readable, honest about timeline feasibility, specific risk calls with CSV evidence

content.text:
Upload your Discovery Assessment Report (PDF or Markdown) and your Current State Architecture Diagram (.drawio or .png).

Also paste your key findings summary in the text box — specifically your answers to the four messy reality questions raised by Sarah and Michael.

rubric.criterion_label: Summarises the four messy reality items (SRV-086/087, APP-027, MSSQL-03, SRV-076) with specific findings from the CSV data
Identifies and quantifies the EOL OS and CRITICAL patch risk with specific server IDs

content.text:
Paste your key findings summary here. Address the four specific questions Sarah and Michael raised:

1. What are SRV-086 and SRV-087? What is the risk?
2. Is APP-027 still in use? What is your recommendation?
3. What is MSSQL-03 and what should be done about it?
4. What is the significance of SRV-076 hosting both ORCL-04 and ORCL-05?

content.headline: Design the AWS environment that Tilington will migrate into — and make it regulator-proof.
content.body:
Discovery is complete. Sarah Williams has approved your findings. The Architecture Review Board is confirmed for 14 April.

Now you must design the target AWS structure — the multi-account organisation, the governance guardrails, the identity model, and the architecture decisions that will underpin everything that follows.

Emma Clarke has already told you she wants written confirmation — not verbal — that no customer PII will leave eu-west-2 under any scenario. That answer needs to be in your ARB pack.

content.text:
## Your Tasks

### Task 1 — High-Level Design (HLD)

Write the HLD document covering:

- **Solution overview** with architecture principles applied
- **AWS account structure** — minimum 6 accounts (Management, Security, Logging, Shared Services, Production, Non-Production) with OU hierarchy and rationale
- **SCP strategy** — write at least 3 SCPs in actual JSON: deny eu-west-2 exit for PII, deny public S3 buckets, deny disabling CloudTrail
- **Identity model** — IAM Identity Centre with Azure AD federation, 6 roles defined
- **Governance controls** — Control Tower guardrails, tagging strategy, budget alerts

### Task 2 — Low-Level Design (LLD)

The LLD must contain specific, implementable detail:

- Per-account service list and SCPs applied
- IAM role policies with trust conditions for at least one cross-account role
- At least 3 SCPs written in full JSON — not described in prose
- Technical mechanism addressing Emma Clarke's data residency requirement

### Task 3 — Architecture Decision Records

Add at least 3 new ADRs to `/docs/adrs/` in the GitHub repo:

- **ADR-003** — eu-west-2 as primary region (UK GDPR rationale)
- **ADR-004** — Terraform as primary IaC (vs CloudFormation-only)
- **ADR-005** — EKS for container orchestration (EKS vs ECS — document the trade-off)

Each must follow the Nygard format: Context, Decision, Consequences, Alternatives Considered.

### Task 4 — Landing Zone Architecture Diagram

Draw the target AWS organisation in Draw.io showing: Organisation root → OUs → Accounts, Control Tower in management account, each account with primary services, and cross-account trust relationships.

### Anti-Fake Validation

- SCPs must be written in valid JSON — describing them in prose scores zero
- The EKS vs ECS ADR must include Tilington-specific trade-offs, not generic statements
- Emma Clarke's data residency answer must name a specific AWS control mechanism

rubric.criterion_label: AWS account structure — 6+ accounts, OU hierarchy, rationale for each account's separation
SCPs written in valid JSON — minimum 3 SCPs with correct deny structure
Identity and access model — IAM Identity Centre, Azure AD federation, 6-role hierarchy
ADRs — minimum 3 in Nygard format with Tilington-specific trade-offs
Data residency — written technical mechanism naming the specific AWS control

content.text: Upload your HLD, LLD, and Landing Zone Architecture Diagram.

sections.title: Your SCPs and Data Residency Answer

rubric.criterion_label: 3 SCPs in valid JSON syntax with correct Effect, Action, Resource structure
Written data residency mechanism answers Emma Clarke's specific question with a named AWS control

content.text:
Paste your 3 SCPs in JSON here, then provide the written data residency confirmation for Emma Clarke.

Emma's question: *Confirm that no customer PII will leave eu-west-2 under any scenario, including DR failover to eu-west-1. What technical controls enforce this? What happens if the control fails?*

content.headline: Design the network and security architecture that keeps Tilington's data safe and the regulator satisfied.
content.body:
The Landing Zone is approved. Now you must design the network that connects Tilington's two datacentres to AWS, and the security controls that satisfy Emma Clarke, Priya Sharma, and the NCSC Cloud Security Principles.

There is also a cost anomaly in the January AWS report that James Patel has flagged. It looks like a monitoring problem — but it is actually an architectural problem. Identifying the correct root cause is part of this phase.

content.text:
## Your Tasks

### Task 1 — Complete the Terraform Networking Module

Complete all TODOs in `/terraform/networking/main.tf`:

- Private application subnets (3 AZs)
- Private data subnets (3 AZs)
- NAT Gateway(s) — justify your HA vs cost decision in a comment
- Route tables: public (→ IGW) and private (→ NAT GW)
- VPC Flow Logs with CloudWatch destination
- Security groups for ALB, EKS nodes, and Aurora — all least-privilege

**The VPC CIDR must not conflict with:** DC1 (10.10.0.0/16), DC2 (10.20.0.0/16), or non-prod (10.40.0.0/16).

Security group rule: SF-004 shows port 22 open to 0.0.0.0/0 on the EKS node security group. This must be fixed — replace SSH with AWS SSM Session Manager.

### Task 2 — Network Architecture Diagram

Draw the full network architecture showing:

- DC1 and DC2 connectivity to AWS (Direct Connect vs VPN — make a justified recommendation citing the $380/month Direct Connect cost from the January report)
- VPC with 3 tiers across 3 AZs
- Transit Gateway for multi-account networking
- VPC gateway endpoints for S3 and DynamoDB
- Route 53 private hosted zones

### Task 3 — Security Architecture Document

Complete `/docs/security-requirements.md` into a full security architecture covering:

- Configuration for each required service: CloudTrail, Config, GuardDuty, Security Hub, KMS, WAF, Macie
- KMS CMK strategy — how many keys, for what purpose, rotation policy
- All 14 NCSC Cloud Security Principles mapped to specific AWS controls — no blanks permitted

### Task 4 — Cross-AZ Cost Analysis

James flagged two figures from the January cost report:

- CloudWatch DataProcessing: $89.40
- AWS Data Transfer Regional: $156.30

One is a monitoring verbosity issue. One is an architectural problem with a free fix.

Identify which is which, name the root cause, and document the fix. Calculate the annual saving.

### Task 5 — Incident Response: SF-002 and SF-018

Respond to Emma Clarke's urgent message. Provide:

- Confirmation that public access has been blocked on the S3 bucket
- Assessment of ICO notification requirement under UK GDPR Article 33 (72-hour clock)
- Data lineage explanation of how NI numbers reached a public bucket
- The governance fix: which SCP or AWS Config rule prevents recurrence

rubric.criterion_label: Private subnets, NAT GW, route tables, VPC flow logs all completed — no 0.0.0.0/0 ingress on any security group except ALB
All 14 NCSC principles mapped to specific AWS controls — no blanks
Cross-AZ root cause: Data Transfer Regional ($156.30) correctly identified as architectural issue — not CloudWatch. VPC gateway endpoints named as the free fix with annual saving calculated
SF-002/SF-018 response: ICO 72-hour assessment made, data lineage explained, governance fix named

content.text: Paste your completed Terraform networking module here (main.tf with all TODOs resolved).

rubric.criterion_label: Correctly identifies Data Transfer Regional ($156.30) as the architectural problem — not CloudWatch ($89.40)
Names S3 gateway endpoint and DynamoDB gateway endpoint as the free fix, calculates $1,875.60 annual saving
Emma Clarke incident response: ICO Article 33 assessment, data lineage, governance fix

content.text:
**Part 1 — Cross-AZ Cost Analysis**

Which line item is the real problem and why? What is the fix? What is the annual saving?

**Part 2 — Response to Emma Clarke (SF-002 / SF-018)**

Provide your written response addressing: public access block confirmation, ICO notification assessment, data lineage, and the governance fix.

content.headline: Build the infrastructure. Write real code. Resolve three live incidents.
content.body:
The architecture is approved. Now it must be built.

The starter repo has incomplete Terraform modules, Python tools with TODO functions, and three operational incidents waiting for you. Every deliverable in this phase requires working code pushed to GitHub.

Three incidents need your attention: an active SSH brute force attack, a cross-AZ cost anomaly with a free fix, and an untagged EC2 instance costing $98.40/month with an unknown owner.

content.text:
## Your Tasks

### Task 1 — Complete the Terraform Repository

Complete all TODOs across the three Terraform modules:

**Networking module** — must `terraform plan` with 0 errors using `-var="environment=non-production"`. Remote state backend configured in S3. All outputs populated (private_app_subnet_ids, private_data_subnet_ids, nat_gateway_ids).

**Security/IAM module** — tighten the KMS key policy from root wildcard to specific principals. Add S3 Object Lock in **COMPLIANCE mode** on the CloudTrail bucket (not GOVERNANCE — explain the difference in a comment). Add S3 bucket policy denying all puts except from CloudTrail service.

**EKS module** — add OIDC provider for IRSA, at least one managed node group, and the aws-load-balancer-controller addon.

Push all Terraform to GitHub. Include `terraform plan` output as evidence.

### Task 2 — Complete the Python Inventory Tool

Complete all TODO functions in `/python/inventory-tool/inventory.py`:

- `get_rds_inventory()` — must identify Oracle instances (engine `oracle-ee` or `oracle-se2`)
- `get_s3_inventory()` — must flag unencrypted buckets and public buckets as HIGH severity
- `get_eks_inventory()` — must flag public API server endpoints

Run the tool and commit actual CSV output to `/python/inventory-tool/sample-output.csv`.

### Task 3 — Complete the Python Cost Reporting Tool

Complete `get_cost_by_service()` and `get_cost_by_tag()` in the cost reporting tool.

Your cost analysis must identify that **Data Transfer Regional at $156.30/month is the architectural problem** — not CloudWatch at $89.40. Fix with S3 and DynamoDB VPC gateway endpoints (free). Annual saving: $1,875.60.

### Task 4 — Complete CloudFormation Templates

Complete all TODOs in `/cloudformation/vpc.yaml`. Create a new `/cloudformation/security.yaml` that enables Security Hub, AWS Foundational Security Best Practices standard, CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark, and GuardDuty.

### Task 5 — Incident Resolution Report

Document all three incidents:

**Incident 1 — SSH Brute Force (SF-006):** Root cause. Containment. Long-term fix (SSM Session Manager replacing SSH). Evidence.

**Incident 2 — Cross-AZ Cost Anomaly:** Root cause (architectural, not monitoring). Fix (VPC gateway endpoints). Annual saving ($1,875.60). Evidence.

**Incident 3 — Untagged EC2 (i-0nonprod003, $98.40/month):** Investigation steps. Owner identified or instance terminated. Governance fix: name the SCP or AWS Config rule that prevents recurrence.

rubric.criterion_label: terraform plan output shows 0 errors across all modules
CloudTrail S3 uses Object Lock COMPLIANCE mode (not GOVERNANCE) with explanation of the difference
Python inventory tool produces actual CSV output with Oracle instances (engine oracle-ee/oracle-se2) correctly identified
CloudFormation security.yaml creates Security Hub with FSBP and CIS standards enabled plus GuardDuty detector
All 3 incidents resolved with root cause, containment, remediation, and evidence — untagged instance governance fix named

content.text: Paste your `terraform plan` output here showing 0 errors. Include the key section showing resource changes across all three modules.

rubric.criterion_label: Incident 1 (SSH brute force): root cause identified, SSM Session Manager named as the long-term fix
Incident 2 (cross-AZ cost): Data Transfer Regional correctly identified, VPC endpoints named, $1,875.60 annual saving calculated
Incident 3 (untagged instance): investigation documented, governance fix (SCP or Config rule) named

content.text:
Document all three incidents using this structure for each:

**Root Cause** — what caused this issue?

**Immediate Action** — what did you do to contain it?

**Long-term Fix** — what prevents recurrence?

**Evidence** — what confirms it is resolved?

content.headline: Decompose Tilington's legacy customer platform into four cloud-native microservices and deploy them to EKS.
content.body:
The IaC platform is operational. Now you must decompose Tilington's legacy Customer Platform into four microservices and deploy them to EKS using GitOps principles.

The Payments Service is in PCI DSS scope and must be network-isolated. The Audit Service must be append-only with no TTL — FCA record-keeping requirements mean audit records cannot be automatically deleted.

The starter Kubernetes files are 15–20% complete. The Helm chart has values defined but no templates. The ArgoCD application points at a cluster URL that doesn't exist yet.

content.text:
## Your Tasks

### The Four Microservices

| Service | Data Store | Regulatory Scope |
|---------|-----------|------------------|
| Customer Service | Aurora PostgreSQL | FCA — PII |
| Payments Service | Aurora PostgreSQL + DynamoDB PaymentEvents | PCI DSS |
| Notifications Service | DynamoDB NotificationDelivery | None |
| Audit Service | DynamoDB AuditTrail | FCA — append-only, no TTL |

---

### Task 1 — Kubernetes Manifests for All 4 Services

For each microservice create: `deployment.yaml`, `service.yaml`, `hpa.yaml`, `pdb.yaml`, and `serviceaccount.yaml`.

Every deployment must include:

- Security context: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true`, `allowPrivilegeEscalation: false`
- Topology spread constraints across 3 AZs (`topology.kubernetes.io/zone`)
- Resource limits and requests
- Liveness and readiness probes
- IRSA annotation on ServiceAccount: `eks.amazonaws.com/role-arn`
- PodDisruptionBudget with `minAvailable: 2`

Complete `ingress.yaml` with ACM certificate ARN placeholder, WAF ACL ARN, and HTTP to HTTPS redirect.

**PCI scope:** Payments Service must be in a separate namespace or have NetworkPolicy restricting inbound to only the ALB and Customer Service.

### Task 2 — Helm Chart Templates

Create actual Helm templates using the values already defined in `values.yaml`:

`templates/deployment.yaml`, `templates/service.yaml`, `templates/hpa.yaml`, `templates/ingress.yaml`, `templates/serviceaccount.yaml`, `templates/pdb.yaml`

The chart must pass `helm template .` without errors — paste the output as evidence.

### Task 3 — ArgoCD GitOps Configuration

- Fix `application.yaml` with a real EKS cluster URL format and correct overlay path
- Create `/gitops/overlays/production/kustomization.yaml` and `/gitops/overlays/non-production/kustomization.yaml`
- Create an `ApplicationSet` at `/gitops/argocd/applicationset.yaml` that templates an Application for all 4 microservices

### Task 4 — Service Boundary and API Design

Write `/docs/service-boundaries.md` — for each service: what data it owns, what it reads from other services via API, what events it publishes, what events it subscribes to.

Write `/docs/api-design.md` — the Customer Service REST API with method, path, request/response JSON schema, HTTP status codes, and PII fields flagged.

sections.title: Submit Your Helm Chart and ArgoCD Config

rubric.criterion_label: All 4 deployments have correct security context (runAsNonRoot, readOnlyRootFilesystem, allowPrivilegeEscalation: false), topology spread constraints, and IRSA ServiceAccount annotation
helm template produces valid YAML without errors — output pasted as evidence
ArgoCD ApplicationSet iterates over all 4 microservices, production and non-production overlays created
PCI scope: Payments Service isolated via separate namespace or NetworkPolicy
Service boundaries document: all 4 services with owns/reads/publishes/subscribes defined, no shared databases

content.text: Paste your `helm template .` output here to prove the chart produces valid YAML. Then paste your ArgoCD ApplicationSet YAML.

rubric.criterion_label: Explains how PCI DSS isolation for the Payments Service is implemented technically
Explains why the Audit Service DynamoDB table must have NO TTL and names the FCA regulatory basis
Explains IRSA and why every ServiceAccount needs the role-arn annotation

content.text:
Answer these three questions:

1. How have you isolated the Payments Service for PCI DSS compliance? What specific Kubernetes mechanism did you use?

2. The Audit Service writes to DynamoDB. Why must this table have NO TTL attribute? What regulation requires this and what is the consequence of setting a TTL?

3. What is IRSA and why does every microservice ServiceAccount in this simulation need the `eks.amazonaws.com/role-arn` annotation?

content.headline: Fix a broken pipeline, build a secure delivery process, and define the SLOs that prove Tilington meets FCA operational resilience requirements.
content.body:
The microservices platform is deployed. Now you must build the delivery pipeline and observability stack.

The GitHub Actions pipeline in `/.github/workflows/deploy.yml` has 10 documented bugs. Find and fix all of them. BUG-004 is the most subtle — the security scan job appears to run but never actually blocks the pipeline.

You will also define SLOs for all four services. The Payments Service has a 99.95% availability SLO — its error budget is 21.6 minutes per month. This number is what Emma Clarke will use to demonstrate FCA PS21/3 operational resilience compliance.

content.text:
## Your Tasks

### Task 1 — Fix All 10 Pipeline Bugs

For each bug: identify the root cause, show the specific lines changed, explain why the original code failed.

| Bug | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| BUG-001 | ECR_REGISTRY env var is empty |
| BUG-002 | docker build uses wrong context path |
| BUG-003 | No Trivy image scan on built image |
| BUG-004 | security-scan job never blocks the pipeline *(most subtle — read how `needs:` and `if:` interact when a job is skipped)* |
| BUG-005 | AWS credentials via long-lived access keys — not OIDC |
| BUG-006 | IMAGE_TAG variable never defined |
| BUG-007 | deploy-production has no AWS credentials step |
| BUG-008 | No rollback step on failed deployment |
| BUG-009 | No smoke test after deployment |
| BUG-010 | No notification on pipeline failure |

### Task 2 — Security Scanning Evidence

Run Trivy against a real Docker image and commit the JSON output to `/ci-cd/security-scan-results/trivy-scan-output.json`.

Run Checkov against the Kubernetes manifests and commit to `/ci-cd/security-scan-results/checkov-k8s-scan.txt`.

For each FAILED Checkov check: document what it flagged, why it matters, and what you changed to fix it.

### Task 3 — Grafana Cloud Dashboards

Create 3 dashboards in Grafana Cloud free tier (grafana.com/products/cloud):

- **Dashboard 1** — Service Health: request rate, error rate, P95 latency for all 4 services
- **Dashboard 2** — Reliability and SLO: SLO compliance, error budget remaining, burn rate
- **Dashboard 3** — EKS Infrastructure: node CPU/memory, pod scheduling, HPA events

Export each as JSON and commit to `/observability/grafana/dashboards/`.

### Task 4 — SLO Framework and Reliability Runbook

Define SLOs for all 4 services:

| Service | Availability SLO | P99 Latency | Error Rate |
|---------|-----------------|-------------|------------|
| Customer Service | 99.9% | < 500ms | < 0.1% |
| Payments Service | 99.95% | < 250ms | < 0.05% |
| Notifications Service | 99.5% | < 2000ms | < 0.5% |
| Audit Service | 99.9% | < 1000ms | < 0.1% |

Calculate the Payments Service error budget: 30 × 24 × 60 × 0.0005 = **21.6 minutes/month**. Show your working.

Define fast burn alert threshold and slow burn alert threshold. Define the escalation path from alert to Sarah Williams. Explain how this framework provides evidence for FCA PS21/3 operational resilience.

rubric.criterion_label: All 10 bugs fixed with root cause and specific lines changed — BUG-004 correctly explains needs:/if: interaction
BUG-005 fixed with OIDC role-to-assume — no long-lived credentials in secrets
BUG-008 and BUG-009: rollback step added using kubectl rollout undo, smoke test added checking /health/ready endpoint
Real Trivy scan output committed (not mocked), Checkov failed checks documented with remediation

content.text: Paste your fixed `deploy.yml` here with each BUG-001 through BUG-010 annotated as a comment showing what was wrong and what you changed.

rubric.criterion_label: Payments Service error budget correctly calculated as 21.6 minutes/month with working shown (30 × 24 × 60 × 0.0005)
Fast burn and slow burn alert thresholds defined with specific percentages and time windows
SLO framework explicitly connected to FCA PS21/3 operational resilience requirements

content.text:
**SLO Error Budget Calculations**

Show your working for the Payments Service error budget. Then calculate error budgets for the other three services.

**Alert Thresholds**

Define your fast burn alert (triggers a page) and slow burn alert (triggers a ticket) with specific thresholds.

**FCA PS21/3 Connection**

How does this SLO framework provide evidence for FCA operational resilience compliance? What would Emma Clarke show the FCA regulator?

content.headline: Design the data layer that replaces Oracle, serves the microservices, and keeps Tilington FCA-compliant for the next decade.
content.body:
The platform is live. Now you must design the databases that power the four microservices and plan the migration path from Oracle.

The Oracle renewal decision deadline is Q2 2026. Sarah needs a recommendation this week. ORCL-01 has 847 stored procedures, 214 of which require manual conversion. The most complex package is `customer_ledger_pkg`.

Two constraints that cannot be negotiated: the AuditTrail DynamoDB table must have NO TTL, and Aurora customer data cannot replicate to eu-west-1 (Ireland).

content.text:
## Your Tasks

### Task 1 — Complete the Aurora PostgreSQL Design

Complete all TODOs in `/databases/aurora-design.md`:

- Instance class selection **justified against the ORCL-01 workload** — not against SRV-006 server specs. Aurora manages memory differently to a bare-metal Oracle server.
- Complete SQL schema for `customers`, `accounts`, and `transactions` tables with all columns, constraints, and indexes
- Oracle migration specifics: list the PL/SQL features in `customer_ledger_pkg` that require manual conversion. Name the DMS task configuration for ORCL-01 → Aurora.
- Backup retention period with FCA record-keeping justification

### Task 2 — Design All 3 DynamoDB Tables

Complete all TODOs in `/databases/dynamodb-design.md`:

**PaymentEvents** — resolve the hot partition problem on the `status` GSI. The `PENDING` partition will receive all writes initially — propose write sharding or an alternative concrete solution.

**NotificationDelivery** — design from scratch: partition key, sort key, 2 GSIs with query patterns documented, TTL set to 90 days.

**AuditTrail** — design from scratch with **NO TTL** (FCA COBS record-keeping — some audit records must be retained indefinitely). Use a separate KMS key `alias/tilington-audit`. Access model: audit-service write-only, compliance-viewer read-only.

### Task 3 — Backup and Recovery Strategy

Write `/docs/backup-recovery-strategy.md`:

- Aurora: backup retention with FCA justification. **Payments Service needs 15-minute RPO** — Aurora automated backups alone do not achieve this. Aurora Point-in-Time Recovery (PITR) does.
- Cross-region backup constraint: Aurora customer data cannot replicate to eu-west-1 (UK GDPR). Propose the solution.
- DynamoDB: PITR for AuditTrail. No Global Tables for AuditTrail (UK data residency).

### Task 4 — Oracle Renewal Recommendation

Write a one-page recommendation for Sarah Williams:

- Name **ORCL-01, ORCL-02, ORCL-03** as the Q2 2026 migration candidates (migrate these before the renewal decision)
- Address Michael Evans's PL/SQL complexity warning honestly (847 stored procedures, 214 requiring manual conversion)
- Give a **specific recommendation** — renew or do not renew — with explicit risk acknowledgement
- Quantify: £1.4M/year Oracle cost → £4.2M saving over 3 years if not renewed

rubric.criterion_label: Aurora instance sizing justified against ORCL-01 workload (not SRV-006 server specs), SQL schema complete with indexes
PaymentEvents hot partition solved with a concrete mechanism — write sharding or equivalent proposed (not just flagged)
AuditTrail has NO TTL with FCA COBS justification, separate KMS key alias/tilington-audit, correct access model
Payments Service 15-minute RPO achieved via PITR specifically (not general backups), cross-region PII constraint resolved
Oracle recommendation names ORCL-01/02/03, addresses PL/SQL complexity honestly, gives a specific recommendation with £4.2M saving quantified

content.text:
Provide your database design decisions here:

**Aurora PostgreSQL** — instance class selection with justification. Key schema decisions.

**DynamoDB PaymentEvents** — how did you resolve the hot partition problem?

**DynamoDB AuditTrail** — confirm NO TTL and explain the FCA basis.

**Payments Service RPO** — how does PITR achieve 15-minute RPO? Why don't daily automated backups achieve this?

**Oracle Recommendation** — renew or do not renew? Give your recommendation with reasoning.

content.headline: Plan the migration, design the DR architecture, and validate that the programme is on budget.
content.body:
The platform is built and the databases are designed. Now you must plan how Tilington's 28 applications migrate across 5 waves, design the disaster recovery architecture, and produce a FinOps assessment from real cost data.

The January AWS cost report contains the data you need. The total spend must be calculated from the CSV — not estimated. One line item looks like a monitoring problem but is actually an architectural issue with a free fix worth $1,875.60/year.

The AuditTrail DynamoDB table must NOT appear in the DR replication plan — FCA audit records must remain in eu-west-2 (UK) only.

content.text:
## Your Tasks

### Task 1 — Complete the Migration Strategy

Complete all TODOs in `/migration/migration-strategy.md`:

- Assign all 28 applications to a wave with reasoning
- APP-012 (Authentication Service) must be Wave 2 — 25 applications depend on it
- Define entry and exit criteria for all 5 waves
- Resolve every application that has `???` in the wave candidates CSV

### Task 2 — FinOps Assessment

Analyse the January 2026 AWS cost report CSV and produce `/finops/finops-assessment.md`.

**Calculate the total January spend** by summing the `blended_cost_usd` column. Do not estimate — sum the actual data.

Identify and quantify all 5 specific findings:

1. **Aurora writer** `db.r6g.2xlarge` at $2,180.16/month — is this right-sized? Compare to ORCL-01 server (SRV-006: 32 vCPU, 256GB RAM). Aurora manages memory differently — justify your conclusion.
2. **Direct Connect** at $380/month — cost justified against the programme budget?
3. **Non-prod EKS nodes** running 24/7 — 2 nodes at $28.08/month each. Saving if scheduled off nights/weekends: $28.08 × 2 × (5/7) ≈ $40/month.
4. **Untagged instance** i-0nonprod003 at $98.40/month — governance issue and cost visibility gap.
5. **Cross-AZ data transfer** $156.30/month — free fix via VPC gateway endpoints. Annual saving: $1,875.60.

### Task 3 — DR Architecture

Design and diagram the DR architecture:

- **Primary:** eu-west-2 (all production workloads and all customer PII)
- **DR:** eu-west-1 (NO customer PII — UK GDPR constraint)
- Aurora: PITR snapshots only — no live PII replica in eu-west-1
- **DynamoDB AuditTrail: excluded from replication entirely** — FCA audit records must remain in UK jurisdiction
- Route 53 health checks and failover routing
- EKS warm or cold standby in eu-west-1

### Task 4 — RTO/RPO Matrix

For each of the 4 services: FCA requirement, target achieved, AWS mechanism, and any gap with remediation.

Payments Service 15-minute RPO must be evidenced with Aurora PITR as the specific mechanism.

### Task 5 — AWS Well-Architected Review

Self-assess against all 5 pillars. Minimum 2 evidence-based risks per pillar drawn from the security findings CSV and cost report. Include remediation for each.

sections.title: Submit Your FinOps Assessment and DR Design

rubric.criterion_label: Total January spend calculated from CSV sum — not estimated. Top 5 costs by service identified.
All 5 specific FinOps findings identified and quantified — cross-AZ $156.30 identified as architectural issue with $1,875.60 annual saving
AuditTrail excluded from DR replication with UK data residency and FCA rationale
Payments Service 15-minute RPO evidenced via Aurora PITR — not general automated backups
All 28 applications assigned to waves with APP-012 in Wave 2 — wave entry and exit criteria defined

content.text:
**January AWS Spend Total** — provide your calculated sum from the CSV (not an estimate).

**5 FinOps Findings** — for each: finding, amount, recommendation, and saving where applicable.

**DR Architecture Summary** — explain how you handle the Aurora PII constraint and why AuditTrail is excluded from replication.

**Payments Service RPO** — how does Aurora PITR achieve 15-minute RPO? Why don't daily backups achieve this?

content.headline: Defend your full AWS architecture in front of the Board. Answer the CRO's questions in writing. Leave with a portfolio that gets you hired.
content.body:
You have completed the full technical programme. Now you must synthesise everything into board-level governance deliverables and defend your architecture at the Architecture Review Board.

The ARB panel: Emma Clarke (CRO), Priya Sharma (Head of Information Security), David Okafor (Head of Retail Technology), and the CFO.

Emma Clarke has sent you three specific questions. These must be answered in writing in the ARB pack — not verbally on the day. The CFO will ask about the Oracle saving and the January AWS spend. Have the numbers ready.

content.text:
## Your Tasks

### Task 1 — ARB Presentation Pack (12–16 slides)

Create a PowerPoint or Google Slides ARB pack. Required slides:

1. Programme title and presenter
2. Executive summary — what was built, why, outcomes achieved
3. AWS architecture overview diagram
4. Multi-account structure and governance model
5. Security posture — Security Hub findings closed, NCSC compliance status
6. **Written answer to Emma Clarke Question 1** — data residency during DR failover
7. Operational resilience — RTO/RPO matrix and DR design
8. Migration progress — wave plan status
9. FinOps — January spend total from CSV, budget vs actual, savings achieved
10. Oracle renewal recommendation for the CFO
11. Outstanding risks — honest summary
12. Next steps to DC exit

### Task 2 — Answer Emma Clarke's Three Questions in Writing

Every answer must name specific technical mechanisms — not prose assertions.

**Question 1 — Data residency during DR failover to eu-west-1:**

Name: the SCP preventing eu-west-1 PII use, the Aurora snapshot-only strategy, the DynamoDB AuditTrail exclusion, and the CloudTrail detective control if the SCP is bypassed.

**Question 2 — Operational resilience during an AWS eu-west-2 availability event:**

Cover: Multi-AZ within eu-west-2 (AZ failure), eu-west-1 warm standby (region failure), Route 53 health check failover mechanism, and what happens to in-flight payments during failover.

**Question 3 — Who has administrative access to the AWS Production account:**

Name: specific IAM Identity Centre roles with Production account access, the break-glass procedure, CloudTrail coverage of all privileged actions, and the monthly access review cadence.

### Task 3 — Architecture Decision Log

Compile every ADR into a single table at `/docs/architecture-decision-log.md`: ADR number, title, status, date, decided by, and one-sentence summary. Note any ADRs that were revised or superseded.

### Task 4 — Risk and Trade-Off Summary

Document accepted risks (with stakeholder sign-off) and the 5 key trade-offs from the programme:

- NAT Gateway: 1 vs 3 (cite the ~£90/month per additional gateway figure)
- Aurora instance class: right-sized vs Oracle server parity
- Direct Connect vs VPN for hybrid connectivity
- Rehost vs refactor timing for APP-002 Trade Processing Engine
- Public vs private EKS API server endpoint

### Task 5 — Final Portfolio Summary

Write a one-page portfolio summary at `/docs/final-portfolio-summary.md` for your CV and LinkedIn. Name specific AWS services, quantify outcomes, and map skills to UK financial services roles paying £70,000–£125,000.

rubric.criterion_label: ARB deck has 12+ slides, references January spend total from CSV (not estimated), Oracle saving of £4.2M over 3 years quantified
Emma Clarke Q1 (data residency): names SCP, Aurora snapshot-only strategy, AuditTrail exclusion, and CloudTrail detective control
Emma Clarke Q2 (operational resilience): covers Multi-AZ, eu-west-1 warm standby, Route 53 failover, and in-flight payment handling
Emma Clarke Q3 (privileged access): names IAM Identity Centre roles, break-glass procedure, and monthly review cadence
Risk and trade-off summary documents all 5 required trade-offs with cost figures — NAT Gateway figure cited

content.text: Upload your ARB presentation deck (PDF or PPTX).

rubric.criterion_label: Q1 data residency: names all 4 mechanisms — SCP, Aurora snapshot-only, AuditTrail exclusion, CloudTrail detective control
Q2 operational resilience: covers Multi-AZ, eu-west-1 failover, Route 53, and in-flight payments
Q3 privileged access: names specific IAM Identity Centre roles, break-glass procedure, and review cadence

content.text:
Write your three answers to Emma Clarke's ARB questions here. These answers will be assessed as if Emma Clarke is reading them herself.

Remember: specific technical mechanisms only. Prose assertions like 'we have controls in place' will score zero.

**Q1 — Data Residency During DR Failover**

**Q2 — Operational Resilience During eu-west-2 Outage**

**Q3 — Administrative Access to Production Account**

content.summary_text:
You have completed the Tilington plc Cloud Modernisation Programme — a full enterprise AWS Solutions Architect simulation covering 9 phases of real work across discovery, architecture, IaC, Kubernetes, CI/CD, databases, migration, and executive governance.

Your GitHub repository at github.com/alain-Sortnext/tilington-cloud-modernisation now contains your complete portfolio — 23 deliverables demonstrating every skill required for mid-senior AWS Solutions Architect roles in UK financial services paying £70,000–£125,000.
skills_earned: AWS Multi-Account Architecture — Organizations, Control Tower, SCPs
HLD and LLD Documentation for Enterprise AWS Workloads
VPC Design, Transit Gateway, and Hybrid Cloud Connectivity
IAM, KMS, GuardDuty, Security Hub — Regulated Security Architecture
Terraform IaC — Networking, Security, and EKS Modules
Kubernetes/EKS — Manifests, Helm, Autoscaling, Security Contexts
GitOps — ArgoCD ApplicationSet and Kustomize Overlays
GitHub Actions CI/CD with DevSecOps — Trivy, Checkov, OIDC
Observability — Grafana Cloud, Prometheus, SLO and Error Budget Design
Aurora PostgreSQL — Design, Oracle Migration, PITR
DynamoDB — Access Pattern Design and Hot Partition Resolution
Cloud Migration Strategy — 6Rs, Wave Planning, Cutover and Rollback
Disaster Recovery Architecture — RTO/RPO Design and FCA Operational Resilience
FinOps — Cost Explorer Analysis, Savings Plans, Cost Optimisation
Architecture Review Board Presentation and Executive Communication
FCA PS21/3, UK GDPR, PCI DSS, and NCSC 14 Principles Compliance
target_roles: AWS Solutions Architect at UK Tier-1 Bank — £75,000–£110,000 (Lloyds, HSBC, Barclays, NatWest)
Senior Cloud Architect at UK Insurance or Asset Management — £72,000–£95,000
Cloud Solutions Architect at UK FinTech — £90,000–£130,000 plus equity
AWS Cloud Architect at Big-4 Consultancy — £80,000–£140,000 (Accenture, KPMG, Deloitte)
AWS Cloud Architect Contract — £600–£750 per day

items: Enterprise AWS architecture is never just technical. Every design decision at Tilington was driven by a regulatory constraint (FCA PS21/3, UK GDPR), a business deadline (Q3 2027 DC exit), or a stakeholder requirement (Emma Clarke's written data residency confirmation). Real architects start with constraints, not services.

The messy reality of legacy estates is the interview differentiator. Finding SRV-086/087, resolving the MSSQL-03 mystery, identifying the ORCL-04/05 shared server on SRV-076, and investigating APP-027 demonstrate discovery skills that UK banks actually need — and that cannot be faked with textbook knowledge.

IaC discipline is non-negotiable in regulated financial services. Every control Emma Clarke required — immutable CloudTrail logs, KMS CMKs, Object Lock in COMPLIANCE mode, tagged resources — must be in Terraform or CloudFormation. ClickOps in a regulated environment is an audit failure waiting to happen.

Cost anomalies are architectural problems, not monitoring problems. The January report showed CloudWatch DataProcessing at $89.40 as the apparent issue, but Data Transfer Regional at $156.30 was the real root cause. Free VPC gateway endpoints eliminated $1,875.60 per year. Real FinOps work requires reading the data correctly.

The completion phase of any cloud programme is governance, not technology. The ARB presentation, Emma Clarke's written answers, the architecture decision log, and the risk and trade-off summary are what regulators, boards, and senior stakeholders actually read. Building excellent infrastructure that you cannot explain in board language is not enough.

skills: AWS Multi-Account Architecture and Landing Zone Design
Enterprise Security Architecture — FCA, PRA, NCSC Compliance
Infrastructure as Code — Terraform and CloudFormation
Kubernetes and EKS Platform Engineering with GitOps
Cloud-Native Database Architecture — Aurora PostgreSQL and DynamoDB
Cloud Migration Strategy — 6Rs and Wave Planning
Disaster Recovery Architecture and FCA Operational Resilience
Architecture Review Board Governance and Executive Communication
